description: Given an array of integers, find two numbers that add up to a specific target number.
title: Two Sum

answers.explanation: Use a hash map to store seen values and return the first matching pair.
Reverse the pointers in one pass using three references.

description: Determine if a string is a valid parenthesis combination.
title: Valid Parentheses

answers.explanation: Use a stack to match each closing bracket against the most recent opening bracket.

description: Find the maximum sum of any contiguous subarray.
title: Maximum Subarray

answers.explanation: Kadane's algorithm keeps the best running sum ending at each position.

description: Merge two sorted arrays into one sorted array.
title: Merge Sorted Array

answers.explanation: Fill nums1 from the back so elements are never overwritten early.

description: Check if a string is a palindrome (ignoring spaces and punctuation).
title: Valid Palindrome

examples: Input: "A man, a plan, a canal: Panama" → Output: true

answers.explanation: Use two pointers and ignore non-alphanumeric characters while comparing in place.

constraints: s consists of printable ASCII characters

description: Implement binary search on a sorted array.
title: Binary Search

answers.explanation: Binary search halves the search space on every step.

constraints: nums is sorted in ascending order

description: Find all unique combinations of numbers that sum to a target.
title: Combination Sum

answers.explanation: Use backtracking and reuse the same candidate while exploring combinations.

description: Implement a LRU (Least Recently Used) Cache.
title: LRU Cache

examples: LRUCache lruCache = new LRUCache(2); lruCache.put(1, 1); lruCache.get(1) → 1

answers.explanation: LinkedHashMap with access order gives a compact LRU implementation.

description: Flatten a nested list of integers into a single list.
title: Flatten Nested List Iterator

answers.explanation: Flatten lazily using a stack of iterators so nested lists are expanded only when needed.

constraints: nestedList consists of nested lists of integers
The values of all integers are in the range [-10^6, 10^6]
